title: Monster Beverage Stock Breaks Out; Florida Builder Surges Past Buy Point
text: Monster Beverage scored a breakout above a buy point Friday, hitting a new high. Shares remain in the 5% buy zone.

title: Monster Beverage (MNST) Up 5.2% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
text: Monster Beverage (MNST) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock?

title: Monster Beverage: Strong Earnings Forecast, Bullish Chart Pattern
text: Monster Beverage Corp. (NASDAQ: MNST) is holding steady in a flat base with an 8% correction, after hitting resistance at $52.75, following a 2-for-1 stock split. The stock began trading on an adjusted basis on March 28, but that didn't affect the chart pattern that's forming.

title: 7 Cash-Rich Companies That Can Weather Any Storm
text: With global market and economic fears once again rising to the forefront, investors may want to consider cash-rich companies that can weather any storm. Fundamentally, enterprises that carry plenty of green in their pockets should be able to navigate whatever curveballs the gatekeepers of the economy decide to throw.

title: Monster Beverage (MNST) Rises 30% in a Year: More Upside Left?
text: Despite elevated costs and currency woes, Monster Beverage's (MNST) continued momentum in the energy drinks category and a robust pipeline of new products are likely to drive growth.

title: The Monster Stock-Split Stock Nobody Is Talking About
text: Monster Beverage isn't getting nearly as much attention for its recently announced stock split. The company has been the best-performing stock over the past 20 years, surging 155,000%.

title: Monster Beverage Stock Is Splitting -- Is It A Buy?
text: Monster Beverage will execute a 2-for-1 stock split at the end of March 2023. The company has been a great growth story and should continue to grow steadily.

title: 10 S&P 500 Stocks Are Practically Immune To More Fed Rate Hikes
text: Is the Fed done jacking up interest rates or not? Angst over that question is torturing the stock market — but a handful of S&P 500 stocks aren't worried.

title: Monster Beverage (MNST) Dips on Q4 Earnings & Sales Miss
text: Monster Beverage's (MNST) Q4 results reflect gains from a strong brand performance, innovation, product launches and pricing actions amid elevated logistic and input costs.
